name: Balatro
short_description: The poker roguelike. Balatro is a hypnotically satisfying deckbuilder where you play illegal poker hands, discover game-changing jokers, and trigger adrenaline-pumping, outrageous combos.
detailed_description:
Balatro is a poker-inspired roguelike deck builder all about creating powerful synergies and winning big.

Combine valid poker hands with unique Joker cards in order to create varied synergies and builds. Earn enough chips to beat devious blinds, all while uncovering hidden bonus hands and decks as you progress. You’re going to need every edge you can get in order to reach the boss blind, beat the final ante and secure victory.

  • The definitive poker roguelike experience.

  • Endless possibilities - every pick-up, discard and joker can dramatically alter the course of your run.

  • In-depth campaign mode complete with 8 difficulties, as well as challenge and seeded runs.

Deftly deploy an array of potent tools: different Decks, Jokers, Tarot cards, Planet cards, Spectral cards, and Vouchers. Use these versatile instruments to ignite a combo frenzy and turbocharge your poker hands, crafting electrifying, adrenaline-fueled synergies.

  • 150 Jokers, each with their own unique abilities

  • 15 Decks with distinct modifiers to choose from for each run

  • 5 potential variants of each Joker which add further abilities

  • 22 Tarot cards variants that can enhance your regular playing cards

  • 11 Planet cards that help level up your possible poker hands

  • 32 Voucher cards to help provide permanent buffs for your current run

Immerse yourself in Balatro’s distinctive psychedelic world. Let the synthwave soundtrack wash over you - wind down, escape the daily grind and prepare to enter the ultimate roguelike flow state.

  • Improve your collection and discover new cards and secrets with every single run.

  • Make use of the in-depth stats screen to study your best hands, most used Jokers, and more!

  • Immerse yourself in the CRT fuzz and enjoy detailed, hand-crafted pixel art.

supported_languages: English, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Dutch, Japanese, Polish, Portuguese - Brazil, Simplified Chinese, Spanish - Latin America, Traditional Chinese

reviews.review_text:
Balatro is excellent, addictive, and one of the most infuriating games I have played in a long time.

When it works, it feels brilliant. Building a run, finding synergy, watching your score explode, and barely surviving into something broken is incredibly satisfying. The core gameplay loop is strong enough that I keep coming back even when I probably should not.

But people should be warned: this game is absolutely designed in a way that can piss you off.

A lot of the difficulty feels good. Some of it does not. There are boss blinds and RNG moments that can completely brick a run you spent a long time building, and sometimes there is little or nothing you can do about it. You can play well, build smart, and still get hit by the exact counter your run cannot survive. That is not every loss, but it happens often enough that it becomes part of the game’s identity.

So yes, I recommend it. It is clever, satisfying, and very easy to sink hours into.

But I would not call it relaxing, and I would not call all of it fair-feeling. This is a game that will sometimes make you feel like a genius, and other times make you feel like it exists purely to waste your time and test your patience.

Great game. Also a game that can be deeply annoying.
Simple yet very enjoyable. Easy to grasp but endless hours of playtime

I wasn't sure if I would like Balatro, with the cards, and deck building and other stuff I usually don't care for in games. I couldn't imagine why a poker game became so popular. Well now I know. This game has got cards and deck building and other stuff I really have come to enjoy. I thought it was going to be too complex for my dumb brain, but I was delighted at how easy to pick up, addictive and charming this game is.
